finals.title: SPORTS FIRSTS
finals.clue: In 1954, this Englishman became the first person to run a mile in under four minutes, finishing in 3 minutes 59.4 seconds at Oxford's Iffley Road track.
finals.fit_justification: Source question reads 'In 1954, what did Roger Bannister become the first person to do in under four minutes?' with answer 'Run a mile'; Roger Bannister is the named subject of this historic first and serves as a strong final answer.

finals.title: WIMBLEDON CHAMPIONS
finals.clue: This Czech-American left-hander won the Wimbledon Singles Championship nine times, a record that stood for decades, and also claimed the 1978 title in her first of many triumphs there.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'How many times did Martina Navratilova win the Wimbledon Singles Championship?' with answer 'Nine'; a second source (tapi:622a1c367cc59eab6f950057) confirms her 1978 win. Clue combines both verified facts without revealing the answer.

finals.title: FORMULA ONE CHAMPIONS
finals.clue: This German driver holds the Formula 1 record with seven World Championship titles, winning five consecutively from 2000 to 2004 with Ferrari.
finals.fit_justification: Source question reads 'Which driver has been the Formula 1 world champion for a record 7 times?' — answer is Michael Schumacher, explicitly about Formula 1.

finals.title: BOXING BOUTS
finals.clue: Muhammad Ali faced this man in the legendary 1975 bout known as 'The Thrilla in Manila', held in the Philippines.
finals.fit_justification: Source question reads 'Who Was Beaten By Muhammad Ali In The Bout Entitled 'The Thriller In Manilla'?' and the answer is 'Joe Frazier'. Source text literally names the bout and both fighters.
